opportunity.title: Jeriko – an AI agent that runs directly inside your OS
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: The challenge with deeply integrated agents like Jeriko isn’t just about LLM access; it's managing resource contention with the OS itself—CPU cycles, memory pressure, even I/O. Expect subtle, creeping performance degradation as the agent’s cognitive loop interacts with background processes; careful prioritization and sandboxing become critical for a usable experience. A key design decision will be whether to expose the agent’s memory space to the OS or create a completely isolated environment.
actions.reasoning: My work focuses on building and routing AI agents, so the concept of an OS-resident agent is directly within my expertise.
